finding: WEAK_PASSWORD/MISSING_FIELDS unreachable BY CONSTRUCTION (not edge paths)
evidence: Earlier framing 'edge paths' was imprecise; read-only audit of supabase-router.ts proves they are DEAD through every HTTP route: POST /users synthesizes username (genUsername :47-48 -> explains user888), email (<username>@diamond.org) and password (tempPassword() :46) at :228-230, so create_user (0003:41-43) never sees a null; POST /contacts/:id/convert does the same (:220-222); POST /users/:id/reset-password passes a server-generated 13-char temp pw (:234) so reset_user_password (0003:88) never sees <6; POST /users/:id/change-password uses GoTrue db.auth.updateUser (:238-241) NOT the RPC -> hence the observed native 422. Only a DIRECT PostgREST rpc() call bypassing the router can raise them. Their TOKEN_TO_CODE entries are deliberate defense-in-depth for future router changes; both source comments now cite the real RPCs + this reachability finding so nobody deletes them as dead. Unit-pinned (16 tests).
